raw_consumer_statement: I booked a home to rent using XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXX/XX/year> with a host named XXXX and XXXX in XXXX, XXXX. On XX/XX/year> I contacted the hosts letting them know that XXXX XXXX had cancelled our flights to XXXX. I told her that I would try and rebook the flights but I might need to cancel. The host, XXXX, told me " no worries, we are easy going ''. I told her that I would try and rebook the flights on this day and there were no flights available with XXXX, so I cancelled the trip within XXXX hours of letting her know there was an issue to not tie up their house. They only refunded me half of the amount of the booking because they say that their cancellation policy is within XXXX hours of booking, but I did let them know within XXXX hours of booking and she in no way said I needed to get it cancelled or I'd be charged. I was trying to see if we could make it work and not tie up their house, but I couldn't. She told me they were flexible and I believed that meant I had another XXXX hours to see if I could get the tickets. The total charge was for {$7000.00} and they refunded {$3600.00}. I am left with a charge of {$3300.00}. I contacted American Express to dispute this charge and provided them with all the information and they closed the dispute in XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  favor. I understood after messaging the host that I had another XXXX hours and would be refunded the total charge and I explained all this to American Express, but they still won't remove the charge.
